fields.name: ERICA HOGAN
fields.feedback: When choosing a company that offers a service that is to secure your business, and properties it can become overwhelming with different options to protect your business. The quality of service we received from Norpro

fields.name: CORY REID
fields.feedback: We use Norpro's service to protect our precious metals from theft. The best thing about the service was the quick communication and the in-depth reports provided to us on a monthly basis. What made the service even better was the quick response time to in

fields.name: Anne Louise
fields.feedback: The security service displayed a high level of professionalism in their approach to securing our premises. Their staff was well-trained and conducted themselves in a manner that was both courteous and vigilant.

fields.name: Marie Eve
fields.feedback: We appreciated the prompt response to our security needs. They were quick to address any arising issues and were always available for immediate assistance.

fields.title: INDUSTRIAL GUARD (FULL-TIME)
fields.description: NORPRO Security is seeking vigilant individuals to join us as Industrial Security Guards. If you're dedicated to ensuring the safety of industrial facilities, assets, and personnel, this role is your opportunity to make a lasting impact.

fields.title: SECURITY GUARD - Healthcare Facility
fields.description: NORPRO Security is on the lookout for compassionate individuals to join us as Security Guards dedicated to healthcare facilities. If you're committed to ensuring the safety of patients, staff, and medical environments, this is your chance to play a vital role in healthcare security.

fields.title: MOBILE PATROL SECURITY GUARD (FULL-TIME)
fields.description: NORPRO Security Ltd. is looking for independent and motivated individuals to join our team as Mobile Patrol Security Guards. Norpro Security Ltd. offers a wide range of security services in Northern Ontario. Our security group administers stand-alone and integrated services to clients ranging from one-time security guards to the complete package that utilizes all of our services. We are proud to have many First Nation partnerships and successful business relations.
fields.schedule:
Job Title: Mobile Patrol Security Guard, NORPRO Security Ltd.
Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time, Days and Nights Shift
Location: Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario
# of Positions: # 1
fields.licence_certification:
• Valid Security Guard License
• Valid Standard First Aid/CPR Certificate
• Valid Class G Driver’s License, Clear Drivers Abstract
• Minimum one year experience in a security guard role
• Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check
fields.responsibilities:
• Patrol property, monitoring surveillance equipment, and inspecting property, equipment and access points, permitting or refusing entry.
• Patrol designated property to monitor and prevent intrusion, damage, hazards and breaches of security.
• Maintain high visibility to deter illegal and inappropriate actions looking for signs of crime or other hazards, taking action to minimize damage and reporting any incidents to their supervisor, clients and emergency services as appropriate
• Observe, identify and report incidents at assigned client sites and will be responsible for the security and safety of clients’ properties.
• Respond to alarms.
• Operate monitoring equipment and complex security cameras.
• Adhere to all company service and operating standards.
• Remain in compliance with local, provincial and federal regulations.
• Immediately respond to emergencies to provide necessary assistance.
• Write and submit security reports.
• Ensure all incident/accident reports are recorded accurately and submitted to the Operations Manager as they occur.
• Provide support, coverage when required, and supervision of Static Security Guards with regular patrol visits and communication.
fields.qualifications:
Required:
• Valid Security Guard License
• Valid Standard First Aid/CPR Certificate
• Valid Class G Driver’s License, Clear Drivers Abstract
• Minimum one year experience in a security guard role
• Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check
Preferred:
• High School Diploma or equivalency.
• Must have demonstrated proficiency with computers and Microsoft programs.
• Above average English oral and written communication skills.
• Basic Computer Skills competency in Microsoft Office and other related programs.
• Ability to respond and deal with urgent/critical issues.
• Work with little or no supervision.
• Certified Non-Violent Crisis Intervention/Use of Force Certification (Or willing to train)
• Certified Resistance Management and Effective Communications. (Or willing to train)
Competencies
Adaptable - Adapts well to changes in assignments and priorities; adapts behavior or work methods in response to new information, changing conditions, or unexpected obstacles; approaches change positively and adjusts behaviors accordingly. be organized, have a keen eye for detail, trustworthy, and able to manage others effectively.
Communication - Clearly conveys and receives information. Keeps others informed as appropriate. Demonstrates good written, oral, and listening skills.
Ability to handle situations in a calm and professional manner.
Decision Making/Problem Solving- Breaks down problems into components and
recognizes interrelationships; makes sound, well-informed, and objective decisions.
Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work independently and make sound decisions under pressure.
Negotiation – Effectively exploring alternatives and positions to reach agreements and
solutions that gain the support and acceptance of all parties.
Positive and professional attitude, with strong customer service skills.
fields.expect:
Working conditions
While performing the duties of this position, the employee may be exposed to weather conditions from 30 degrees to below 20 degrees and exposed to various climates and conditions. Wearing safety clothing and equipment may at times be required.
Physical requirements
While performing the duties of this position, the employee is regularly required to drive, sit, walk, bend for extended periods; reach with hands and arms; talk and listen on a regular basis.
Must be able to perform under pressure, concentrate for a prolonged period and able to work long hours under stressful conditions. The employee may be exposed to the risks associated with attempting to resolve issues with difficult staff, other people.
At Norpro Security Ltd. you can expect:
• Extended Health Benefits, Dental and Vision
• RSP Match Contributions
• Employment growth opportunity
• Professional development opportunities
• Being a part of a team and company that appreciates each other and your efforts.


Apply by sending your resume and cover letter to hiring@norpro.ca by October 15th, 2024, 4:00 pm.
Please state in Subject Line: Mobile Patrol Security Guard


Norpro Security Ltd. is committed to building a diverse workforce representative of the communities we serve. We encourage and are pleased to consider applications from all qualified candidates, without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital/family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or disability.
Norpro Security Ltd. is an inclusive employer. Accommodation is available in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005. Individuals requiring accommodation during the application and/or the interview process should contact Human Resources as soon as possible to make appropriate arrangements.
Only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted. An offer of employment will be conditional upon an acceptable Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC) Record Check.
Our company and our employees’ success go hand in hand. Are you ready to join our team?

fields.title: Industrial Security Guard (Permanent Full Time)
fields.description:
NORPRO Security Ltd. is looking for independent and motivated individuals to join our team as Industrial
Security Guards. Norpro Security Ltd. offers a wide range of security services in Northern Ontario. Our
security group administers stand-alone and integrated services to clients ranging from one-time security
guards to the complete package that utilizes all of our services. We are proud to have many First Nation
partnerships and successful business relations.


Job Type: Permanent, Full-Time
Wage: Starting at $21.16, and receive up to $22.45 within a year
# of Positions: Multiple available
Location: Various Locations in Northern Ontario
fields.schedule:
• 12/13/14 hour shift – 7 days / 7 days off rotation
• Day shift
• Evening shift
• Night shift
• Weekend availability
fields.licence_certification:
• Security Guard Licence (required)
• Valid Full G Driver's Licence (required)
• Valid First Aid / CPR Certification (required)
Work Location: In person, on mine site
Application deadline: ongoing
We also provide free shuttle* to and from the worksite, free meals and accommodation and uniforms.
(*free shuttle is from Sault Ste. Marie base location)
fields.responsibilities:
As an Industrial Security Guard, your key duties and responsibilities include:
• Provide security by protecting people and property.
• Conduct routine patrols with your team.
• Ensure the security, safety and well-being of all personnel, visitors, and the premises.
• Immediately respond to emergencies to provide necessary assistance.
• Follow procedures for various initiatives, including fire prevention, property patrol, traffic control
and accident investigations.
• Write and submit security reports.
• Ensure all incident/accident reports are recorded accurately and submitted to the Manager as
they occur.
fields.qualifications:
• Candidates must be willing to do shift rotational work (7 days on-site -7 days off or 14 days on-
site- 14 off)

• Must hold a valid Security License, Valid  Full G Driver’s License and valid First Aid CPR Certificate. Must have Clear Driver's Abstract and Clear Police Background Check
• Must be computer literate and able to write electronic reports.
• Excellent verbal and written English communication skills.
• You must be physically capable of extended patrolling, have strong interpersonal skills and
customer service orientation.
fields.expect:
At Norpro you can expect:
• Competitive wages starting at $21.16 per hour.
• Room (private) and meals at the mine site are covered.
• Employment growth opportunity.
• Expert training.
• Opportunity for free training for the Certified Protection Officer (CPO) course with a guaranteed
$1 raise for successful candidates.
• A diverse and flexible combination of sites and schedules.
• Being a part of a team and company that appreciates each other and your efforts.
Please send a copy of your Resume, Cover Letter, Security Guard License, Valid Driver’s Licence and Valid First Aid
Certificate to our email at hiring@norpro.ca
We are committed to build a workforce that reflects the communities we serve and to promote a diverse,
anti-racist, inclusive, accessible, merit-based, respectful and equitable workplace.
We invite all interested individuals to apply and encourage applications from from all qualified candidates,
without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital/family status, sexual orientation, gender
identity, age or disability.
Norpro Security Ltd. is an inclusive employer. Accommodation is available in accordance with the Ontario
Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005. Individuals requiring
accommodation during the application and/or the interview process should contact Human Resources as
soon as possible to make appropriate arrangements.
Only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted. An offer of employment will be conditional upon
an acceptable Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC) Record Check.


If you do not have your security license, we can help you get it! Click here and learn more about our Industrial Guard Training Academy.
fields.place: In Various Locations across Northern Ontario

fields.title: SECURITY GUARD - Healthcare Facility
fields.description: NORPRO Security is on the lookout for compassionate individuals to join us as Security Guards dedicated to healthcare facilities. If you're committed to ensuring the safety of patients, staff, and medical environments, this is your chance to play a vital role in healthcare security.

fields.title: Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator
fields.description:
In this role, the Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator will effectively represent and market the Norpro Security Ltd. brand, our business objectives, and career opportunities to potential candidates. They are responsible for attracting suitable candidates for open job positions and assessing their relevant knowledge and experience. The Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator remains up-todate on the requirements of the various positions in the company. The Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator reports to the HR Manager.

Job Type: Permanent. Full-Time

Number of Position: 1

Location: Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario.
fields.schedule:
*Mondays to Fridays- 8:30am to 4:30pm

Work will be in an office environment and may periodically require travel off site and out of town.
fields.responsibilities:
● Source and implement innovative recruiting strategies to attract the best talent.
● Source and build the candidate network using both traditional and innovative recruiting
strategies to attract both active and passive candidates.
● Attract suitable candidates through databases, online employment forums, social media, etc.
● Build strong, diverse candidate pipelines and develop relationships with candidates for future
opportunities.
● Create and publish job ads in various portals.
● Network with potential hires through professional groups on social media and during events
● Assess applicants' knowledge, skills, and experience to best suit open positions.
● Schedule and conduct applicant interviews in an efficient and professional manner.
● Conduct background checks on potential new employees
● Complete paperwork for new hires.
● Manage and administer employee onboarding processes such as new hire package
creation/distribution, conducting orientations, drafting announcements, collecting applicable
documents, etc.
● Provide recruitment reports to team managers.
● Keep up to date on current employment legislation and regulations and enforcing them within
the company.
● Monitor recruitment practices to ensure compliance with applicable provincial employment
and human rights legislation.
● Work collaboratively with HR team and all departments within the organization.
● Adhere to all company policies, procedures and business ethics codes and ensures that they
are communicated and implemented within the team.
fields.qualifications:
● Post-Secondary degree/diploma in Business Administration or Human Resources
● The ability to conduct different types of interviews.
● Experience with recruitment processes and databases.
● The ability to design and implement recruiting strategies.
● A working knowledge of employment law and legislation
● Valid Driver' License, Clear Drivers Abstract and must complete a criminal background check
(CPIC)
fields.expect:
Competencies
Adaptable-
● Adapts well to changes in assignments and priorities; adapts behavior or work methods in
response to new information, changing conditions, or unexpected obstacles; approaches
change positively and adjusts behaviors accordingly.
● Superior organization skills with a keen eye for detail.
● Trustworthy and ability to handle confidentiality and sensitive information.
● Must be able to work well under pressure and multitask.
Communication -
● Clearly conveys and receives information and ideas to individuals or groups in a manner that
engages the listener, helps them understand and retain the message.
● Keeps others informed as appropriate. Demonstrates good written, oral, and listening skills.
● Ability to handle situations in a calm and professional manner.


Decision Making/Problem Solving -
● Breaks down problems into components and recognizes interrelationships; makes sound,
well-informed, and objective decisions.
● Compares data, information, and input from a variety of sources to draw conclusions; takes
action that is consistent with available facts, constraints, and probable consequences.
● Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills
● Ability to work independently and make sound decisions under pressure.

 

Direct Reports 

None

fields.title: Health and Safety Coordinator
fields.description: To provide support to all parties in the workplace. To assist clients to ensure work is being performed in line with any defined procedure/practice and/or legislation.  To promote an overall positive safety culture by working to ensure others complete their work safely, by identifying hazards and making recommendations to correct.
fields.schedule:
Work will be on a construction project in a heavy industrial environment.

Job Type: Temporary, on-call/casual basis with 8–12-hour shifts, days/nights/weekends, overtime shifts.

Number of Positions: 4

Location: Sault Ste. Marie, ON

Start Date: May/June

Closing Date: Until positions are filled.

Physical Requirements:

   Walking, climbing stairs/ladders, standing long periods of time.

   Must be able to lift <50lbs

   Must be able to wear full PPE required for work, consisting of but not limited to:

  • CSA approved hard hat

  • CSA approved steel toe boots.

  • High vis apparel.

  • CSA approved safety glasses.

  • Appropriate hearing protection.

  • Full body harness; working at heights.

  • Respiratory protection (clean shaven) if required.

   At Norpro, you can expect:

  • Competitive wages

  • Employment growth opportunity

  • Professional development opportunities

  • Being a part of a team and company that appreciates each other and your efforts.
fields.responsibilities:
• Conduct regular workplace/site inspections including the ability to monitor and recommend corrective action.

  • Collaborate with Managers and Supervisors to monitor compliance and identify and resolve safety issues.

  • Assist in the development and maintenance of health and safety policies and procedures.

  • Ensure all regulatory requirements are being adhered to including ensuring employees work in compliance with company health and safety standards, in addition to OHSA and applicable regulations.

  • Execute and/or coordinate internal investigations to determine root causes and actions to prevent.

  • Deliver safety orientation for all new and existing employees, when required.

  • Conduct hazard assessments and risk assessments on equipment and processes.

  • Perform job site inspections, recording findings on documents and distributed to Clients as required.

  • Attend health and safety project site meetings, take notes to bring information back to work group.

  • Maintain SDS information and perform follow-ups on job site inspections.

  • Conduct audits and make recommendations on personal protective equipment.

  • Analyze the relevant leading and lagging indicators to take or recommend appropriate actions to prevent a reoccurrence.

  • Participate in various projects as they relate to the health and safety function of the Client.

  • Manage all relevant and related administrative duties as required by the Company/Client.

  • Travel to various mine sites/industrial worksites may be required.

  • Other duties as required.
fields.qualifications:
• Relevant post-secondary training in Occupational Health and Safety or a combination of education and experience is determined to be equivalent.

  • A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Health and Safety professional is preferred.

  • Professional Health and Safety designation such as Canadian Registered Safety Professional (CRSP), Certified Health and Safety Consultant (CHSC) or working towards designation is an asset.

  • Possession of a valid class G licence is required.

  • Joint Health and Safety Certification is an asset.

  • Working at Heights (WAH) is an asset.

  • Standard First Aid with CPR and AED Certification is an asset.

  • Bilingualism (English and French) is an asset.

  • Self-starter with excellent organizational and time management skills

  • Attentive to details to ensure quality.
fields.expect:
• Demonstrates strong leadership qualities and is a team player.

  • Experience in a related Health and Safety position within the construction and/or mining industry with knowledge of Occupational Health and Safety Act and Regulations.

  • Knowledgeable and highly competent health and safety professional who is passionate about making a difference in people's lives.

  • Hard worker who cares about the well being of themselves and others.

  • Familiar with WSIB regulations and policies.

  • Good understanding of current legislation and regulations as it pertains to health and safety.

  • Ability to provide detailed reports and develop safety procedures.

  • Good understanding of data analysis and risk assessment.

  • Proficiency with database applications and administrative systems, specifically Microsoft Office and Windows.

  • Strong organizational skills and ability to work alone, prioritize tasks and responsibilities accordingly.

  • Excellent relationship- building and communication skills. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to effectively communicate with internal and external customers.

  • Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines, while maintaining a positive attitude and providing exemplary customer service.

  • Ability to work independently and to carry out assignments to completion within parameters of instructions given, prescribed routines, and standard accepted practices.

      Direct Reports:

      None

 

Please send a copy of your Resume and Cover Letter to  healthandsafety@norpro.ca .

     

We are committed to build a workforce that reflects the communities we serve and to promote a diverse, anti-racist, inclusive, accessible, merit-based, respectful, and equitable workplace.

 

We invite all interested individuals to apply and encourage applications from all qualified candidates, without regard to race, color, citizenship, religion, sex, marital/family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age or disability.

 

Only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted. An offer of employment will be conditional upon an acceptable Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC) Record Check.

fields.title: Shuttle Driver
fields.description:
Transport NORPRO Staff on a bus, van, or other light vehicle between locations. The shuttle Driver is expected to greet customers, help them enter the vehicle, and assist them in loading their luggage, keeping the vehicle neat and making sure it is always in perfect working condition.

 

Job Type: Permanent, Part-Time
Wage: $19.05 per hour 
# of Positions: 1
Location: Various Locations in Northern Ontario
fields.schedule:
• 10-20 Hours per week
• Day shift
• Evening shift
• Night shift
• Weekend availability
fields.licence_certification:
• Valid G License (required)
  • Valid First Aid/CPR Certificate (preferred)
  • Work Location: In person, on mine site. Work will require consistent travel for extended periods of time to various locations to transport staff to work sites.
  • Application deadline: ongoing
fields.responsibilities:
• Convey passengers to and from predetermined locations
  • Assist passengers in loading and unloading their luggage into the vehicle
  • Answer passengers’ questions in a polite and gentle manner
  • Maintain constant communication with the dispatcher or manager
  • Ensure shuttle is fueled, well-maintained and presentable at all time
  • Observe all relevant traffic and safety laws
  • Carry out repair of minor faults and report major problems, accidents, or damage to the vehicle to the dispatcher or technician
  • Obey all traffic rules and regulations guiding the operation of shuttle services in his/her assigned area. This may include plying the predetermined routes, not going beyond stipulated boundaries,
  • Dress in a neat and professional manner.
  • Other related duties as assigned
fields.qualifications:
• Valid G License required
  • Valid First Aid/CPR Certificate preferred
  • Provide and maintain Clear Drivers Abstract and Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check as a condition of employment
  • Working knowledge of pertinent car maintenance techniques
  • A good knowledge of traffic rules and regulations guiding the operation of shuttle drivers in that area is also necessary
  • Able to work flexible shifts
  • Work will require consistent travel for extended periods of time to various locations to transport staff to work sites.
  • Require travelling long distances for extended periods. The nature of the position may expose the shuttle driver to a moderate/high level of tension and anxiety due to the weather conditions, time constraints and demands for the resources.
fields.expect:
Please send a copy of yourResume, Valid Driver’s Licence, and Valid First Aid
Certificate to our email at hiring@norpro.ca
We are committed to build a workforce that reflects the communities we serve and to promote a diverse,
anti-racist, inclusive, accessible, merit-based, respectful and equitable workplace.
We invite all interested individuals to apply and encourage applications from from all qualified candidates,
without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital/family status, sexual orientation, gender
identity, age or disability.
Norpro Security Ltd. is an inclusive employer. Accommodation is available in accordance with the Ontario

accommodation during the application and/or the interview process should contact Human Resources as
soon as possible to make appropriate arrangements.
Only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted. An offer of employment will be conditional upon
an acceptable Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC) Record Check.

fields.title: Full Time Static Security Guard
fields.description:
Job Title: Static Security Guard
Job Type: Permanent Full Time (Union)
Site Location: Arauco
# of Positions: 1
Shift:afternoon, Weekdays and Weekends
Reports to: Operations Manager


NORPRO Security Ltd. is looking for an independent and motivated individual to join our team in the role of
Full Time Static Security Guard.
In this role, the Static Guard will be responsible for providing safety and protection of all employees, visitors, and company assets by maintaining a high visibility presence to deter illegal and inappropriate actions, looking for signs of crime or other hazards, taking action to minimize damage and reporting any incidents to their supervisor, clients, and emergency services as appropriate.
This will include stationary guards, and patrol on foot and monitor premises to prevent theft, violence, or infractions of rules.


Duties and responsibilities
•Patrol designated property to monitor and prevent intrusion, damage, hazards, and breaches of security.
•Observe, identify, and report incidents at assigned client sites and will be responsible for the security and safety of client’s properties.
•Respond to alarms.
•Operate monitoring equipment and complex security cameras.
•Ensure the security, safety and well-being of all personnel, visitors, and the premises.
•Adhere to all company service and operating standards.
•Remain in compliance with local, provincial, and federal regulations.
•Immediately respond to emergencies to provide necessary assistance.
•Write and submit security reports.
•Ensure all incident/accident reports are recorded accurately and submitted to the Manager as they occur.
•Must be able to lift and carry and move 50 pounds regularly. Other related duties as requested.


Qualifications
Required
•Valid Security Guard License.
•Valid Standard First Aid/CPR.
•Valid Full G Driver’s License
•Clear Driver’s Abstract
•Clear Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC)
Preferred
•High School Diploma or equivalency.
•Must have demonstrated proficiency with computers and Microsoft programs.
•Above average English oral and written communication skills.
•Basic Computer Skills competency in Microsoft Office and other related programs.


Competencies
Adaptable - Adapts well to changes in assignments and priorities; adapts behavior or work methods in response to new information, changing conditions, or unexpected obstacles; approaches change positively and adjusts behaviors accordingly. be organized, have a keen eye for detail, trustworthy, and able to manage others effectively.
Communication - Clearly conveys and receives information. Keeps others informed as appropriate. Demonstrates good written, oral, and listening skills.
Ability to handle situations in a calm and professional manner.
Decision Making/Problem Solving- Breaks down problems into components and recognizes interrelationships; makes sound, well-informed, and objective decisions. Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work independently and make sound decisions under pressure.
Negotiation – Effectively exploring alternatives and positions to reach agreements and solutions that gain the support and acceptance of all parties.
Positive and professional attitude, with strong customer service skills.


Working conditions
While performing the duties of this position, the employee may be exposed to weather conditions from 30 degrees to below 20 degrees and exposed to various climates and conditions. Wearing safety clothing and equipment may at times be required.
Hours of work will be made in consideration of work location. The hours of work will be scheduled for coverage over 7 days of the week 24 hours of the day as a 24/7 operation. Employees must be able to work nights and weekends, 36 to 40 hours per week.


Physical requirements
While performing the duties of this position, the employee is regularly required to stand, sit, walk, bend for extended periods; reach with hands and arms; talk and listen on a regular basis.
Must be able to perform under pressure, concentrate for a prolonged period and able to work long hours under stressful conditions. The employee may be exposed to the risks associated with attempting to resolve issues with difficult staff, other persons.


Apply by sending your resume and cover letter to hiring@norpro.ca by, October 15th, 2024, 4pm. Please state job title in Subject Line: Full Time Static Security Guard at Arauco.
Norpro Security Ltd. is committed to building a diverse workforce representative of the communities we serve. We encourage and are pleased to consider applications from all qualified candidates, without regard to race, color, citizenship, religion, sex, marital/family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or disability.
Norpro Security Ltd. is an inclusive employer. Accommodation is available in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005. Individuals requiring accommodation during the application and/or the interview process should contact Human Resources as soon as possible to make appropriate arrangements.
Only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted. An offer of employment will be conditional upon an acceptable vulnerable sector records check.
fields.schedule:
Working conditions
While performing the duties of this position, the employee may be exposed to weather conditions from 30 degrees to below 20 degrees and exposed to various climates and conditions. Wearing safety clothing and equipment may at times be required.
Hours of work will be made in consideration of work location. The hours of work will be scheduled for coverage over 7 days of the week 24 hours of the day as a 24/7 operation. Employees must be able to work nights and weekends, 36 to 40 hours per week.
fields.licence_certification:
Qualifications
Required
•Valid Security Guard License.
•Valid Standard First Aid/CPR.
•Valid Full G Driver’s License
•Clear Driver’s Abstract
•Clear Police Criminal Record and Judicial Matters Check (PCRJMC)
Preferred
•High School Diploma or equivalency.
•Must have demonstrated proficiency with computers and Microsoft programs.
•Above average English oral and written communication skills.
•Basic Computer Skills competency in Microsoft Office and other related programs.
fields.responsibilities:
Duties and responsibilities
•Patrol designated property to monitor and prevent intrusion, damage, hazards, and breaches of security.
•Observe, identify, and report incidents at assigned client sites and will be responsible for the security and safety of client’s properties.
•Respond to alarms.
•Operate monitoring equipment and complex security cameras.
•Ensure the security, safety and well-being of all personnel, visitors, and the premises.
•Adhere to all company service and operating standards.
•Remain in compliance with local, provincial, and federal regulations.
•Immediately respond to emergencies to provide necessary assistance.
•Write and submit security reports.
•Ensure all incident/accident reports are recorded accurately and submitted to the Manager as they occur.
•Must be able to lift and carry and move 50 pounds regularly. Other related duties as requested.

fields.title: Human Resources Co-Ordinator
fields.description:
Job Title: Human Resources Co-Ordinator
Job Type: 1 Year Contract with possibility for extension
Wage: Dependent upon experience Location: Sault Ste. Marie, ON


Norpro Security Ltd. is seeking an independent and motivated individual to join our Human Resources
team for a one-year term contract. Norpro offers quality specialized Security, Safety and Hygiene Services
across Northern Ontario. Norpro Security Ltd. specializes in safeguarding the mining, industrial and
institutional sectors; offering stand-alone and integrated services to clients ranging from one-time
security to a complete package that utilizes all our services. Norpro Health & Safety offers Quantitative
Respirator Fit Testing (at our location or onsite/mobile available), Occupational Hygiene Exposure
Assessments, Program Gap Analysis, Management System Auditing, Program Development and a variety
of Health and Safety Training options.


We are proud of our strong partnerships with First Nations and our successful business relationships. If
you're ready to contribute to a dynamic team, we’d love to hear from you!


.
fields.responsibilities:
As a Human Resources Coordinator your key duties & responsibilities include:


Serve as point of contact for employees to answer routine questions and provides guidance on basic
human resource policies and procedures.
Redirecting HR related calls or distributing correspondence to the appropriate person on the team.
Escalates issues to the Human Resources Manager as appropriate.
Assist with day-to-day operations of the human resources functions and duties.
Support Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator with recruitment, interviews and orientation.
Maintain digital and electronic records of employee’s information by entering and updating data.
Maintain quality service by following organization standards.
Maintain employee confidence and protect operations by keeping human resource information
confidential.
Work closely with management and employees to continuously improve workplace culture, build
morale, increase productivity, and employee engagement and retention.
Work collaboratively with the entire team and all departments within the organization.
fields.qualifications:
Qualifications:
Post Secondary education in Human Resources or Business Administration required
Experience in an HR role or relevant human resources/administrative position
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
Efficient computer skills and competency in Microsoft Office and other related programs
Works well under pressure and meets tight deadlines.
Strong decision-making and problem-solving skills
Valid Class G Driver’s License, Clear Drivers Abstract and must complete a criminal background check
(CPIC)
fields.expect:
Apply by sending your resume to hiring@norpro.ca by October 7, 2024. Please include a copy of your
resume and cover letter. Only those selected for interview will be contacted.


Norpro is committed to creating an inclusive culture by preventing and removing barriers for persons
with disabilities. If you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know
before your interview.

fields.name: Rajneesh Kumar
fields.feedback: I’m originally from India, from a state called Haryana, which is close to Punjab in the northern part of India. I moved to Canada about four and a half years ago as a student and completed my studies in the Police Foundations program at Sault College.

fields.name: Ryan Vendramin
fields.feedback: I loved working for the Norpro Security division. I was there for a little over a year and worked as a loss prevention and patrol officer.  Norpro Security gave me the experience to transition from a security guard to law enforcement.

fields.name: Jean Luc
fields.feedback: I appreciate the strong teamwork within the department. Collaborating with colleagues has been instrumental in efficiently handling emergency situations and implementing preventive measures.

fields.name: Robert Beland

fields.name: Steve Tarchuk
fields.title: Chief Executive Officer
fields.bio: Steve Tarchuk is the CEO of Norpro Security. He brings 17 years of operations management in the steel industry in hot metal movement, blast furnace iron making, and sales along with expertise in above-ground mining operations technology application. Steve is eager to utilize his business experience to continue to grow and develop Norpro across Northern Ontario. Through fostering a culture of collaboration, growth, and mutual respect, Steve strives to create an environment where every employee can thrive and contribute their best to the company's mission.

fields.name: Jordan Gregorini
fields.title: Senior Account Manager
fields.bio: As Norpro’s Senior Account Manager for Northern Ontario, Jordan helps businesses and organizations throughout the North to protect their most important assets: their people and their property. With a combination of military training, police training, and business education, Jordan is uniquely able to both understand clients’ business needs and identify where they are most at risk. By taking the time to carefully listen to clients’ requests, Jordan can effectively develop a custom solution that secures their assets while delivering peace of mind and meeting their budgetary constraints. As a former reserve member of the Royal Canadian Artillery, today Jordan volunteers for the 2310 Royal Canadian Army Cadets, engaging with tomorrow’s leaders and teaching them the skills they’ll need to succeed!

fields.name: Mike Steinburg
fields.title: Manager of Operations
fields.bio: Mike Steinburg is an experienced account manager with 20 years experience in the security industry. He has spent 17 years as an armed guard doing cash in transit, and five years as an account manager. From a management standpoint, Mike takes a hands-on policy, personally guiding his staff towards an organized and understanding approach, where the client comes first – and value-added is an important element on making sure not only himself, but his staff, go above and beyond.

fields.name: Lisa Culligan
fields.title: Human Resources Manager
fields.bio: Lisa Culligan is a Human Resources Leader with over 25 years of experience in Human Resources Management and Occupational Health and Safety in Ontario and Manitoba. Lisa's expertise lies in successfully directing organizational planning and development through strategic initiatives and programs. Lisa is dedicated to fostering effective and positive relations between team members, developing talent through mentorship and coaching, and creating a work environment in which everyone feels both motivated and valued.

fields.name: Aparnna Muttath
fields.title: Human Resources Coordinator
fields.bio: As the Human Resources Coordinator at Norpro, Aparnna plays a pivotal role in maintaining employee records, overseeing training programs, and ensuring compliance with labour laws. With a proactive approach to enhancing employee engagement, Aparnna aligns HR strategies with business objectives, contributing to organizational success. With seven years of professional experience in Dubai, Aparnna's administrative, managerial, and operational background enriches her current role. As member of the Human Resources Professionals Association, Aparnna is dedicated to continuous professional development and is currently pursuing a Certified Human Resources Professionals designation. Armed with a post-graduate certificate in HR and Business Management, Aparnna has undertaken specialized courses from Macquarie University. Passionate about people and organization, Aparnna strives to keep Norpro's HR processes seamless, supporting and empowering the Norpro team for optimal service delivery. Grateful for the collaborative culture and impactful work at Norpro, Aparnna is inspired to contribute to a dynamic team where growth opportunities abound!

fields.name: Leila Kiani
fields.title: Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator
fields.bio: Leila Kiani is the Recruitment and Engagement Coordinator at Norpro. Leila’s focus is to attract top talents through effective recruitment strategies and to enhance employee retention by engaging and making current employees feel valued and connected to the company. Leila’s previous experience involves both recruitment processes and employee engagement activities and she is excited to take this knowledge and apply it to her current role. Her goal at Norpro is to bring together passionate individuals and promote positive relationships between employees and the company. Leila is very excited about the opportunity to turn her goal into reality!

fields.name: Jessica Amadio
fields.title: Industrial Hygiene Specialist
fields.bio: Jessica Amadio is a graduate of the University of Guelph with an Honors Degree in Biological Sciences. With years of occupatonal/industrial hygiene experience, Jessica’s expertise has been focused on assessing worker exposure, health & safety program development and management, and providing H&S training. As the Industrial Hygiene Specialist with Norpro, Jessica is driven by her commitment to protecting workers and promoting a culture of safety, while empowering organizations to create healthier and safer work environments. She is eager to share her passion for health & safety and the importance of industrial hygiene in today’s ever changing work environments with others.

fields.name: Shannan Boston
fields.title: Health and Safety Specialist
fields.bio: As a Health & Safety Specialist with over 20 years’ experience in Occupational Safety, Shannan is passionate about fostering a positive culture and believes with training and mentoring, all employees can improve their own personal safety and increase their hazard awareness. She has worked in both the private sector in Safety Management and the public sector as a Health and Safety Consultant Trainer within the prevention system of Ontario.  As a generalist, she spends her time in the areas of Risk Management, Auditing, Program Development and Training. She is known for her ability to engage creativity within a group, she is very resourceful and values a good challenge when looking for solutions. As an active volunteer with WSPS she assists with planning of local Partners in Prevention Conferences and Events and is proud to call Northern Ontario home.  She has worked with many organizations and communities in both Provincial and Federal Jurisdictions to help them identify areas of opportunity to progress their program to an integrated health and safety management system. Shannan looks forward to utilizing her knowledge and experience paired with the collaboration of the dynamic and supportive team at Norpro to continue to make workplaces safer.

fields.name: Danielle Amadio
fields.title: Communications Manager
fields.bio: Danielle Amadio is the Communications Manager at Norpro, bringing with her years of experience in the communications field. She has extensive knowledge and expertise using social media to develop brand awareness and leverage digital content. Danielle holds a bachelor’s degree in communication studies from Wilfrid Laurier University and has since worked in various communications-based roles in Sault Ste. Marie. Danielle is excited to build the Norpro brand within the community and across Northern Ontario. Her focus is community outreach, creating a strong social media presence, as well as supporting internal and external initiatives. She can’t wait to share what Norpro can do!

fields.name: Debbie Walls
fields.title: Workforce and Logistics Supervisor
fields.bio: Debbie Walls is the Workforce and Logistics Supervisor for Norpro. Debbie has worked at Norpro for four years, in several different roles. In her current role, Debbie’s main responsibility is scheduling Norpro's 118 employees, supervising local static guards and patrol guards, as well as working closely with the Manager of Operations and the rest of the team to ensure seamless, day-to-day operations. Debbie enjoys her role and being a part of the Norpro team! She looks forward to the future as Norpro grows and she is excited to take on new challenges and expand her skill set and knowledge!

fields.name: Melissa Simms
fields.title: Controller
fields.bio: Melissa Simms is the controller at Norpro. She has 13 years experience in the finance industry as well as 7 years combined industrial experience working in labour and quality control positions. Melissa strives to create and maintain healthy relationships with all coworkers, clients, and vendors, and is excited to be a part of the Norpro team.

fields.name: Jim Van Benthem
fields.title: Quality Assurance/Quality Control Lead
fields.bio: Jim Van Benthem is the Quality Assurance/ Quality Control Lead at Norpro. His role within the organization is to ensure that the on-site services provided to clients not only meets their needs but exceeds those requirements as well. This is accomplished through structured training, on-the-job coaching, and well-defined procedures, among other things - all of which are constantly monitored and re-evaluated. Jim has many years of experience as a supervisor and multi-site manager in a highly procedural environment, which allows him to recognize areas for development and improvement. Jim is proud to be part of the Norpro team - from the front-line staff all the way up to the executive management - the support generated at all levels to the QA/QC process will continue to be a growing benefit to Norpro’s valuable clients!

fields.name: Amandeep
fields.title: Operations Supervisor
fields.bio: Amandeep is the Operations Supervisor at Norpro, bringing with him two years of experience with the company. Amandeep began his career with Norpro in July 2022 as a Security Guard and has since progressed through various positions, including Patrol Supervisor and Industrial Guard. Amandeep is enthusiastic about his journey with Norpro and is excited about both the company's growth and the development of his own skills.

fields.title: Lead Hazard Awareness Training
fields.place: The 8 Meeting Room - Norpro Head Office

fields.title: Type 1 & 2 Asbestos Operations Worker Training
fields.place: In Various Locations across Northern Ontario
